title: Ham It Up Day!
story: Every April 15, kitchens across America smell amazing because it's National Glazed Spiral Ham Day! Families gather around dinner tables to enjoy sweet, sticky glazed ham that's been spiral-cut for easy serving. The glaze is usually made with brown sugar, honey, or maple syrup that creates a shiny, caramelized coating. Many people serve this special ham for spring celebrations and family gatherings, making it a delicious centerpiece that everyone can share.
activity_question: What ingredients are commonly used to make the glaze for spiral ham?
activity_choices: Brown sugar, honey, or maple syrup
activity_answer: Brown sugar, honey, or maple syrup
bonus_fact: The spiral cuts make the ham look fancy and help the glaze soak into every slice!

title: Rub Out Your Mistakes Day!
story: Every April 15, people celebrate National Rubber Eraser Day by appreciating those pink bouncy helpers that fix our mistakes! Students bring their favorite erasers to school, from tiny ones shaped like food to giant novelty erasers that look like animals. Teachers often organize eraser art contests where kids create sculptures or drawings using only erasers. Some people collect vintage erasers or trade cool designs with friends. It's the perfect day to remember that making mistakes is okay because we can always erase and try again!
activity_question: What do some teachers organize on National Rubber Eraser Day?
bonus_fact: The pink eraser was invented in 1858 and originally cost more than a pencil!

title: World Art Day: Paint the Planet Happy!
story: Artists everywhere grab their brushes, pencils, and clay on April 15 for World Art Day! Museums open their doors for free, art teachers host special workshops, and kids create masterpieces in schools and community centers. People share their artwork online using hashtags, while street artists paint colorful murals in cities around the globe. Some families visit galleries together, discovering famous paintings and sculptures. Others try new art forms like pottery or digital drawing. It's a day when creativity takes center stage and everyone becomes an artist!
activity_question: What do museums often do on World Art Day to help people celebrate?
follow_up_question: What kind of artwork would you create if you could make anything for World Art Day?

title: Wild Guessing Gets Its Own Day!
story: Every April 15, people celebrate National Take a Wild Guess Day by making their silliest, most outrageous guesses about everything! Friends challenge each other to guess how many jellybeans are in a jar, what their teacher had for breakfast, or how tall the world's biggest pizza might be. The whole point is to throw out your wildest ideas without worrying about being right or wrong. Schools turn it into fun games where kids guess answers to crazy questions. Social media fills up with people sharing their most ridiculous guesses about celebrities, sports scores, and random facts. It's the one day when being completely wrong is totally awesome!
activity_question: What's the main point of National Take a Wild Guess Day?
activity_choices: To always guess the right answer
To make wild guesses without worrying about being wrong
To guess what aliens eat for lunch
activity_answer: To make wild guesses without worrying about being wrong
follow_up_question: What's the wildest guess you could make about something in your classroom right now?

title: Breaking Barriers on the Baseball Diamond
story: Every April 15, baseball fans celebrate Jackie Robinson Day to honor the brave player who broke baseball's color barrier. On this special day, every single player in Major League Baseball wears number 42 - Jackie's jersey number. Fans pack stadiums wearing 42 jerseys too, creating a sea of matching numbers. Schools and communities hold ceremonies sharing Jackie's story of courage and determination. It's a powerful reminder that one person can change the world through bravery and talent.
activity_question: What do all Major League Baseball players wear on Jackie Robinson Day?
bonus_fact: Jackie Robinson was also the first Black player in modern Major League Baseball history.
